text: Agentic investigation runs on deterministic validation tools
textPreview: Agentic investigation runs on deterministic validation tools

text: TIA separates UI, orchestration, deterministic controls, and trading-system evidence so the MVP remains explainable and auditable.
textPreview: TIA separates UI, orchestration, deterministic controls, and trading-system evidence so the MVP remains explainable and auditable.

text: Streamlit UI presents Trading System Validation, Investigative Experience, Investigation Flow, Agents at Work, and Demo Stack.
textPreview: Streamlit UI presents Trading System Validation, Investigative Experience, Investigation Flow, Agents at Work, and Demo Stack.

text: Planner, market/order/engine investigators, regulatory mapper, critic, and synthesizer coordinate the investigation path.
textPreview: Planner, market/order/engine investigators, regulatory mapper, critic, and synthesizer coordinate the investigation path.

text: Deterministic validation agents execute control checks and return PASS, FAIL, or REVIEW with evidence and reason codes.
textPreview: Deterministic validation agents execute control checks and return PASS, FAIL, or REVIEW with evidence and reason codes.

text: Seeded demo evidence or AlgoEngine_TCP gathers order, market-data, engine, admin, and Exchange Simulator observations.
textPreview: Seeded demo evidence or AlgoEngine_TCP gathers order, market-data, engine, admin, and Exchange Simulator observations.

text: Design principle: the agentic layer can choose and explain the path, but deterministic validators remain the authority for control outcomes.
textPreview: Design principle: the agentic layer can choose and explain the path, but deterministic validators remain the authority for control outcomes.

text: How the agentic layer thinks and acts
textPreview: How the agentic layer thinks and acts

text: The investigation behaves like a Trade Solution Analyst: it selects the right path, calls specialists, challenges evidence, and explains the result.
textPreview: The investigation behaves like a Trade Solution Analyst: it selects the right path, calls specialists, challenges evidence, and explains ...

text: Reads testcase, question, and parameters; selects market-first or order-first route.
textPreview: Reads testcase, question, and parameters; selects market-first or order-first route.

text: Market, order, and engine agents run relevant control groups as tools.
textPreview: Market, order, and engine agents run relevant control groups as tools.

text: Links validation evidence back to the active TIA control catalog.
textPreview: Links validation evidence back to the active TIA control catalog.

text: Flags missing, weak, or contradictory evidence before final answer.
textPreview: Flags missing, weak, or contradictory evidence before final answer.

text: Creates final status, confidence, root cause, and remediation.
textPreview: Creates final status, confidence, root cause, and remediation.

text: Starts with crossed, locked, and one-sided market conditions; verifies whether protections restrict or normalize unsafe states.
textPreview: Starts with crossed, locked, and one-sided market conditions; verifies whether protections restrict or normalize unsafe states.

text: Starts with duplicate, bad-price, market-order, parent-child, kill-switch, and frequency controls; verifies unsafe order prevention.
textPreview: Starts with duplicate, bad-price, market-order, parent-child, kill-switch, and frequency controls; verifies unsafe order prevention.

text: Routing is dynamic within controlled boundaries: the planner chooses the path, while the allowed control groups and deterministic validators keep the result testable.
textPreview: Routing is dynamic within controlled boundaries: the planner chooses the path, while the allowed control groups and deterministic validat...

text: How investigative agents call deterministic agents
textPreview: How investigative agents call deterministic agents

text: Specialist investigation agents do not directly decide pass or fail. They request control execution through the toolbox and registry.
textPreview: Specialist investigation agents do not directly decide pass or fail. They request control execution through the toolbox and registry.

text: Receives selected control IDs from market, order, or engine investigation agents.
textPreview: Receives selected control IDs from market, order, or engine investigation agents.

text: Maps control IDs such as CTRL-002 or STRESS-001 to scenario name and validation_agent.
textPreview: Maps control IDs such as CTRL-002 or STRESS-001 to scenario name and validation_agent.

text: Resolves the deterministic validation agent such as duplicate-order-agent or crossed-market-agent.
textPreview: Resolves the deterministic validation agent such as duplicate-order-agent or crossed-market-agent.

text: Executes the deterministic agent against seeded demo evidence or AlgoEngine_TCP and returns structured evidence.
textPreview: Executes the deterministic agent against seeded demo evidence or AlgoEngine_TCP and returns structured evidence.

text: PASS / FAIL / REVIEW, confidence, root cause, reason codes, remediation, evidence table, and agent trace.
textPreview: PASS / FAIL / REVIEW, confidence, root cause, reason codes, remediation, evidence table, and agent trace.

text: Current MVP: rule-driven agentic orchestration over deterministic validation tools. Future enhancement: optional LLM-assisted planner with allow-listed paths and fallback to rules.
textPreview: Current MVP: rule-driven agentic orchestration over deterministic validation tools. Future enhancement: optional LLM-assisted planner wit...
